revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''黄丹'''（おうだん/おうに）とは皇太子の御袍に用いられる黄赤色。絶対禁色のひとつ。 黄丹とは[[鉛丹]]の別名。昇...'
revision.text:
'''黄丹'''（おうだん/おうに）とは皇太子の御袍に用いられる黄赤色。絶対禁色のひとつ。
黄丹とは[[鉛丹]]の別名。昇る朝日の色を写したとされる鮮やかな色である。
== 概要 ==
『[[養老律令]]』「衣服令」において初めて「皇太子礼服 礼服冠 黄丹衣 牙笏 白袴 白帯 深紫紗褶」と皇太子の位色（位に固定された色）として黄丹が登場する。現代でも立太子の礼、婚礼の儀などに皇太子が黄丹袍を着用する。
『[[延喜式]]』によれば、「黄丹綾一疋 紅花大十斤八両 支子一斗二升」と[[ベニバナ]]と[[クチナシ]]を重ね染めした赤味の強いオレンジであることがうかがえる。
== 関連項目 == *[[黄櫨染]] *[[梔子色]] *[[朱華色]] *[[禁色]]
== 参考文献 == *福田邦夫『すぐわかる 日本の伝統色』東京美術 ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 *吉岡幸雄『日本の色辞典』紫紅社 ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{DEFAULTSORT:おうたん}} [[Category:色名]]

'''黄丹'''（おうだん/おうに）は、[[皇太子]]の御袍に用いられる黄赤色。絶対[[禁色]]の1つである。
黄丹という語自体は[[鉛丹]]の別名でもある。昇る朝日の色を写したとされる鮮やかな色である。
== 概要 == 『[[養老律令]]』の「衣服令」において、初めて「皇太子[[礼服 (宮中)|礼服]] 礼服[[礼冠|冠]] '''黄丹衣''' 牙笏 白袴 白帯 深紫紗褶」と、皇太子の位色（位に固定された色）として黄丹が登場する。現代でも[[立太子]]の礼、[[婚礼]]の儀などに、皇太子は黄丹袍を着用する。
『[[延喜式]]』によれば、「黄丹綾一疋 紅花大十斤八両 支子一斗二升」とあり、[[ベニバナ]]と[[クチナシ]]を重ね染めした赤味の強いオレンジであることがうかがえる。
== 関連項目 == *[[黄櫨染]] *[[梔子色]] *[[朱華色]] *[[禁色]]
== 参考文献 == *福田邦夫『すぐわかる 日本の伝統色』東京美術 ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 *吉岡幸雄『日本の色辞典』紫紅社 ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{DEFAULTSORT:おうたん}} [[Category:色名]]

'''黄丹'''（おうだん/おうに）は、[[皇太子]]の御袍に用いられる黄赤色。絶対[[禁色]]の1つである。
黄丹という語自体は[[鉛丹]]の別名でもある。昇る朝日の色を写したとされる鮮やかな色である。
== 概要 == 『[[養老律令]]』の「衣服令」において、初めて「皇太子[[礼服 (宮中)|礼服]] 礼服[[礼冠|冠]] '''黄丹衣''' 牙笏 白袴 白帯 深紫紗褶」と、皇太子の位色（位に固定された色）として黄丹が登場する。現代でも[[立太子]]の礼、[[婚礼]]の儀などに、皇太子は[[黄丹袍]]を着用する。
『[[延喜式]]』によれば、「黄丹綾一疋 紅花大十斤八両 支子一斗二升」とあり、[[ベニバナ]]と[[クチナシ]]を重ね染めした赤味の強いオレンジであることがうかがえる。
== 関連項目 == *[[黄櫨染]] *[[梔子色]] *[[朱華色]] *[[禁色]]
== 参考文献 == *福田邦夫『すぐわかる 日本の伝統色』東京美術 ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 *吉岡幸雄『日本の色辞典』紫紅社 ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{DEFAULTSORT:おうたん}} [[Category:色名]]

黄丹という語自体は[[鉛丹]]の別名でもある。昇る朝日の色を写したとされる鮮やかな色である。
== 概要 == 『[[養老律令]]』の「衣服令」において、初めて「皇太子[[礼服 (宮中)|礼服]] 礼服[[礼冠|冠]] '''黄丹衣''' 牙笏 白袴 白帯 深紫紗褶」と、皇太子の位色（位に固定された色）として黄丹が登場する。現代でも[[立太子]]の礼、[[婚礼]]の儀などに、皇太子は[[黄丹袍]]を着用する。
『[[延喜式]]』によれば、「黄丹綾一疋 紅花大十斤八両 支子一斗二升」とあり、[[ベニバナ]]と[[クチナシ]]を重ね染めした赤味の強いオレンジであることがうかがえる。

{{色}}{{Infobox color|title=黄丹|hex=F47A55| kana=おうだん/おうに |english= | r=244|g=122|b=85| c=0|m=50|y=65|k=4| h=14|s=65|v=96| munsell=-}} '''黄丹'''（おうだん/おうに）は、[[日本]]の[[皇太子]]の[[袍]]に用いられる黄赤色である。昇る朝日の色を写したとされる鮮やかな色である。また、黄丹という語自体は[[鉛丹]]の別名でもある。
皇太子の色としての黄丹は、『[[養老律令]]』の「衣服令」において、初めて「皇太子[[礼服 (宮中)|礼服]] 礼服[[礼冠|冠]] '''黄丹衣''' 牙笏 白袴 白帯 深紫紗褶」と、皇太子の位色（位に固定された色）として登場する。現代でも、[[立太子]]の礼、[[婚礼]]の儀などに、皇太子は[[黄丹袍]]を着用する。『[[延喜式]]』によれば、「黄丹綾一疋 紅花大十斤八両 支子一斗二升」とあり、[[ベニバナ]]と[[クチナシ]]を重ね染めした赤味の強いオレンジであることがうかがえる。皇太子の位色であることから、他人は着用できない[[禁色]]とされた。
== 関連項目 == *[[黄櫨染]] *[[梔子色]] *[[朱華色]]
== 参考文献 == *福田邦夫『すぐわかる 日本の伝統色』東京美術 ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 *吉岡幸雄『日本の色辞典』紫紅社 ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{DEFAULTSORT:おうたん}} [[Category:色名]]

revision.comment: Created page with "{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōtan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōtan''' (黄丹, おうだん/おうに) is a yellow-red color traditionally used for the [[robes]] of Japan's [[Crown Prince]]. It is a vivid color said to reflect the hues of the rising sun. The term "Ōtan" is also a synonym for [[red lead]] pigment. As the Crown Prince's..."
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōtan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōtan''' (黄丹, おうだん/おうに) is a yellow-red color traditionally used for the [[robes]] of Japan's [[Crown Prince]]. It is a vivid color said to reflect the hues of the rising sun. The term "Ōtan" is also a synonym for [[red lead]] pigment.
As the Crown Prince's color, Ōtan first appeared in the ''[[Yōrō Code]]'' ("Code of Attire") as the color of the Crown Prince's [[ceremonial robes]]. The code specifies the attire as: "Crown Prince's [[formal attire]]: [[crown]] with ''Ōtan'' robes, ivory [[scepter]], white trousers, white sash, and deep purple silk overskirt." Even today, the Crown Prince dons ''Ōtan'' robes for ceremonies like the [[Investiture]] and [[wedding ceremonies]]. According to the ''[[Engishiki]]'' ("Procedures of the Engi Era"), ''Ōtan'' dye was made by overlapping [[safflower]] and [[gardenia]] dyes, producing a reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's official color, it was classified as a [[forbidden color]] and could not be worn by others.
== Related Terms == * [[Kōrozan]] * [[Kuchinashi-iro]] * [[Shuiro]]
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Quick Guide to Traditional Japanese Colors,'' Tokyo Bijutsu. ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 * Sachio Yoshioka, ''Japanese Color Dictionary,'' Shikosha. ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{Color-stub}} [[Category:Color names]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōtan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōtan''' (黄丹, おうだん/おうに) is a yellow-red color traditionally used for the [[robes]] of Japan's [[Crown Prince]]. It is a vivid color said to reflect the hues of the rising sun. The term "Ōtan" is also a synonym for [[red lead]] pigment.
As the Crown Prince's color, Ōtan first appeared in the ''[[Yōrō Code]]'' ("Code of Attire") as the color of the Crown Prince's [[ceremonial robes]]. The code specifies the attire as: "Crown Prince's [[formal attire]]: [[crown]] with ''Ōtan'' robes, ivory [[scepter]], white trousers, white sash, and deep purple silk overskirt." Even today, the Crown Prince dons ''Ōtan'' robes for ceremonies like the [[Investiture]] and [[wedding ceremonies]]. According to the ''[[Engishiki]]'' ("Procedures of the Engi Era"), ''Ōtan'' dye was made by overlapping [[safflower]] and [[gardenia]] dyes, producing a reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's official color, it was classified as a [[forbidden color]] and could not be worn by others.
== Related Terms == * [[Kōrozan]] * [[Kuchinashi-iro]] * [[Shuiro]]
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Quick Guide to Traditional Japanese Colors,'' Tokyo Bijutsu. ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 * Sachio Yoshioka, ''Japanese Color Dictionary,'' Shikosha. ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{Color-stub}} [[Category:Color names]] [[Category:Original pages]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōtan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōtan''' (黄丹, おうだん/おうに) is a yellow-red color traditionally used for the [[robes]] of Japan's [[Crown Prince]]. It is a vivid color said to reflect the hues of the rising sun. The term "Ōtan" is also a synonym for [[red lead]] pigment.
As the Crown Prince's color, Ōtan first appeared in the ''[[Yōrō Code]]'' ("Code of Attire") as the color of the Crown Prince's [[ceremonial robes]]. The code specifies the attire as: "Crown Prince's [[formal attire]]: [[crown]] with ''Ōtan'' robes, ivory [[scepter]], white trousers, white sash, and deep purple silk overskirt." Even today, the Crown Prince dons ''Ōtan'' robes for ceremonies like the [[Investiture]] and [[wedding ceremonies]]. According to the ''[[Engishiki]]'' ("Procedures of the Engi Era"), ''Ōtan'' dye was made by overlapping [[safflower]] and [[gardenia]] dyes, producing a reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's official color, it was classified as a [[forbidden color]] and could not be worn by others.
== Related Terms == * [[Kōrozan]] * [[Kuchinashi-iro]] * [[Shuiro]]
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Quick Guide to Traditional Japanese Colors,'' Tokyo Bijutsu. ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 * Sachio Yoshioka, ''Japanese Color Dictionary,'' Shikosha. ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{Color-stub}} [[Category:Color names]] [[Category:Original pages]] [[Category:Kk]][[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Ōni]] to [[Ōdan]]: moving to name that is less prone to confusion with Oni demons

According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

revision.comment: adding original pages categories to all the pages I newly created today
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[Category:dd]][[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Pages needing attribution]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍}}) of the {{ill|Crown Prince of Japan|ja|皇太子}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead tetroxide|ja|鉛丹}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|ja|養老律令}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal attire|ja|礼服}}, formal {{ill|Crown (headgear)|ja|礼冠}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|ja|延喜式}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden color|ja|禁色}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
[[ja:黄丹]]

revision.text:
{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=黄丹 |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''黄丹'''（おうだん/おうに）は、[[日本]]の[[皇太子]]の[[袍]]に用いられる黄赤色である。昇る朝日の色を写したとされる鮮やかな色である。また、黄丹という語自体は[[鉛丹]]の別名でもある。
皇太子の色としての黄丹は、『[[養老律令]]』の「衣服令」において、初めて「皇太子[[礼服 (宮中)|礼服]] 礼服[[礼冠|冠]] '''黄丹衣''' 牙笏 白袴 白帯 深紫紗褶」と、皇太子の位色（位に固定された色）として登場する。現代でも、[[立太子]]の礼、[[婚礼]]の儀などに、皇太子は[[黄丹袍]]を着用する。『[[延喜式]]』によれば、「黄丹綾一疋 紅花大十斤八両 支子一斗二升」とあり、[[ベニバナ]]と[[クチナシ]]を重ね染めした赤味の強いオレンジであることがうかがえる。皇太子の位色であることから、他人は着用できない[[禁色]]とされた。
== 関連項目 == * [[黄櫨染]] * [[梔子色]] * [[朱華色]]
== 参考文献 == * 福田邦夫『すぐわかる 日本の伝統色』東京美術 ISBN 4-8087-0784-5 * 吉岡幸雄『日本の色辞典』紫紅社 ISBN 4-87940-549-3
{{Color-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おうたん}} [[Category:色名]]

皇太子の色としての黄丹は、『[[養老律令]]』の「衣服令」において、初めて「皇太子[[礼服 (宮中)|礼服]] 礼服[[礼冠|冠]] '''黄丹衣''' 牙笏 白袴 白帯 深紫紗褶」と、皇太子の位色（位に固定された色）として登場する。現代でも、[[立太子]]の礼、[[婚礼]]の儀などに、皇太子は[[黄丹袍]]を着用する。『[[延喜式]]』によれば、「黄丹綾一疋 紅花大十斤八両 支子一斗二升」とあり、[[ベニバナ]]と[[クチナシ]]を重ね染めした赤味の強いオレンジであることがうかがえる。皇太子の位色であることから、他人は着用できない[[禁色]]とされた。

'''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|qq=|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince of Japan]]. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for [[:en:Lead(II,IV) oxide|Lead tetroxide]] (鉛丹).

As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|qq=|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|qq=|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}}
* {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|qq=|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}}
* {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}

'''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince of Japan]]. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for [[:en:Lead(II,IV) oxide|Lead tetroxide]] (鉛丹).

As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.

== Related Articles ==
* {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}}
* {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}}
* {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}

== References ==
* Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5.
* Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.

It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for [[:en:Lead(II,IV) oxide|Lead tetroxide]] (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.

As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}

'''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince of Japan]]. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for [[:en:Lead(II,IV) oxide|Lead tetroxide]] (鉛丹).

As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11677717]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince of Japan]]. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for [[:en:Lead(II,IV) oxide|Lead tetroxide]] (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
{{translated page|ja|黄丹|version=61611786|comment=Imported full ja history}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q11677717]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince of Japan]]. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for [[:en:Lead(II,IV) oxide|Lead tetroxide]] (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11677717]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the [[:en:Crown prince|Crown Prince of Japan]]. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for [[:en:Lead(II,IV) oxide|Lead tetroxide]] (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''[[:en:Yōrō Code|Yōrō Code]]'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's [[:en:Formal wear|Formal attire]], formal [[:en:Raikan|Crown (headgear)]], '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''[[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]]'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a [[:en:Forbidden colors (Japan)|Forbidden color]], prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
{{translated page|ja|黄丹|version=61611786|comment=Imported full ja history}}

revision.comment: v25: Convert interwiki links [[:en:...]] to {{ill}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11677717]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:36, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|WD=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince of Japan}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead(II,IV) oxide|en|Lead(II,IV) oxide|WD=Q419205|lt=Lead tetroxide}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal wear|en|Formal wear|WD=Q1151364|lt=Formal attire}}, formal {{ill|Raikan|en|Raikan|WD=Q11588147|lt=Crown (headgear)}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
{{translated page|ja|黄丹|version=61611786|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead(II,IV) oxide|en|Lead(II,IV) oxide|WD=Q419205|lt=Lead tetroxide}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal wear|en|Formal wear|WD=Q1151364|lt=Formal attire}}, formal {{ill|Raikan|en|Raikan|WD=Q11588147|lt=Crown (headgear)}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.

As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color}}, prohibiting its use by others.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]]
{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|WD=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince of Japan}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead(II,IV) oxide|en|Lead(II,IV) oxide|WD=Q419205|lt=Lead tetroxide}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal wear|en|Formal wear|WD=Q1151364|lt=Formal attire}}, formal {{ill|Raikan|en|Raikan|WD=Q11588147|lt=Crown (headgear)}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Rittaishi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Carthamus tinctorius|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:黄丹]]
{{No footnotes|date=2016-10-21}} {{色}} {{Infobox color |title=Ōdan |hex=F47A55 |kana=おうだん/おうに |english= |r=244 |g=122 |b=85 |c=0 |m=50 |y=65 |k=4 |h=14 |s=65 |v=96 |munsell=- }} '''Ōdan''' ({{ill|Yellow cinnabar|ja|黄丹|1=Ōdan|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yellow cinnabar|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōdan|qq=}}, おうだん/おうに) is a vivid yellowish-red color used in the ceremonial robes ({{ill|Hō (garment)|ja|袍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hō (garment)|1=hō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q134436881}}) of the {{ill|Crown prince|en|Crown prince|WD=Q207293|lt=Crown Prince of Japan|1=Crown prince}}. It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead(II,IV) oxide|en|Lead(II,IV) oxide|WD=Q419205|lt=Lead tetroxide|1=Lead(II,IV) oxide}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal wear|en|Formal wear|WD=Q1151364|lt=Formal attire|1=Formal wear}}, formal {{ill|Raikan|en|Raikan|WD=Q11588147|lt=Crown (headgear)|1=Raikan}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Q11597818|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=Wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color|1=Forbidden colors (Japan)}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
{{translated page|ja|黄丹|version=61611786|comment=Imported full ja history}}

It is said to represent the color of the rising sun. The term ''ōdan'' is also a synonym for {{ill|Lead(II,IV) oxide|en|Lead(II,IV) oxide|WD=Q419205|lt=Lead tetroxide|1=Lead(II,IV) oxide}} (鉛丹).
As the Crown Prince's color, ''ōdan'' first appeared in the ''{{ill|Yōrō Code|en|Yōrō Code|WD=Q2575743|lt=Yōrō Code|1=Yōrō Code}}'' under the "Clothing Ordinance," where it was described as the fixed color for the Crown Prince's {{ill|Ceremonial dress|ja|礼服 (宮中)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ceremonial dress|1=Raifuku|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11588163}}: "Crown Prince's {{ill|Formal wear|en|Formal wear|WD=Q1151364|lt=Formal attire|1=Formal wear}}, formal {{ill|Raikan|en|Raikan|WD=Q11588147|lt=Crown (headgear)|1=Raikan}}, '''ōdan robe''' (黄丹衣), ivory {{ill|Scepter|ja|牙笏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scepter|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white {{ill|Hakama|ja|白袴|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hakama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, white belt, and deep purple {{ill|Sha-ju|ja|紗褶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sha-ju|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}." Even today, the Crown Prince wears the ''Ōdan no hō'' (黄丹袍) during ceremonies such as the {{ill|Rittaishi|ja|立太子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rittaishi|1=Q11597818|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11597818}} (Investiture as Crown Prince) and {{ill|Japanese imperial wedding|ja|婚礼|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese imperial wedding|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚式|1=Wedding|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q49836}} ceremonies. According to the ''{{ill|Engishiki|en|Engishiki|WD=Q1342448|lt=Engishiki|1=Engishiki}}'', ''ōdan'' dye was produced using {{ill|Safflower|ja|紅花|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Safflower|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ベニバナ|1=Safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q156625}} and {{ill|Gardenia jasminoides|ja|梔子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia jasminoides|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to クチナシ|1=Gardenia jasminoides|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1072101}}, resulting in a strong reddish-orange hue. As the Crown Prince's exclusive color, ''ōdan'' was designated a {{ill|Forbidden colors (Japan)|en|Forbidden colors (Japan)|WD=Q11591157|lt=Forbidden color|1=Forbidden colors (Japan)}}, prohibiting its use by others.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Kōrozen|ja|黄櫨染|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōrozen|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 黄櫨染御袍}} * {{ill|Gardenia color|ja|梔子色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gardenia color|1=color of safflower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11538718}} * {{ill|Shu-kasa color|ja|朱華色|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shu-kasa color|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 唐棣色|1=Hanezuiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11418576}}
== References == * Kunio Fukuda, ''Suguwakaru Nihon no dentōshoku'' (すぐわかる 日本の伝統色), Tokyo Bijutsu, ISBN 4-8087-0784-5. * Yukio Yoshioka, ''Nihon no iro jiten'' (日本の色辞典), Shikosha, ISBN 4-87940-549-3.
